summary: Remove cross-session overlap penalty from scoreCumulativeRework and filter trivial lines from rework counting
context: Run d18ab582 showed only 4.7pt spread on cumulativeRework across conditions. The overlap penalty (1.5pts per cross-session file touch) penalized coordination conditions MORE because they intentionally work on overlapping files — the opposite of what we want to measure.
rationale: Two changes: (1) Removed cross-session overlap penalty entirely — overlap without rework is coordination working correctly, and the rework ratio already captures uninformed rework. (2) Added trivial line filter (blank lines, lone braces, imports, comments, JSDoc, coordination artifacts) so only substantive code rework is counted. This should increase spread by removing noise.

alternatives.option: Keep overlap penalty but exclude coordination conditions
alternatives.reason_rejected: Condition-aware scoring violates the blinding principle — the scorer shouldn't know which condition produced the output.

alternatives.option: Invert the overlap penalty into a bonus for coordination conditions
alternatives.reason_rejected: Same problem — scorer shouldn't give bonuses based on condition identity. The rework ratio alone is condition-agnostic.
